simply another sample of the
Seattle spirit" says May Ark
wright Hutton of Spokane in re-
gard to the Seattle report that she
is heading an insurgent movement
of east of the mountain members
eof the Washington Equal Suffrage
association. The report has it that
Mrs. Hutton is working to defeat
Mrs. Emma Smith De Voc as state
president when the convention as
,sembles in Seattle June 80.


"We have no designs on the
presidency" Mrs. Hutton stated
today. "All we ask is that some
representative woman who owns
property be elected to the office.
It makes no difference what part
Of the state she comes from. There
is no intention of stirring up tur-
moil among equal suffrage advo-
Gates. BY harmonious action we
can win, but there is nothing to
be gained by discord. The Seattle
spirit that demands everything for
that city seems to be alarmed for
fear it will lose the headquarters
of the suffrage movement, and
there are plenty of equal suffrage
opponents who are willing to help
start fight. That's all there is
TO the report"
